summary: Backend fix for truncated ZIP verified. All 4 pytest tests PASSED. Content-Length header matches actual body size exactly (9128837 bytes = ~9.1MB, no truncation). ZIP integrity confirmed via zipfile.testzip() returning None. All 60 original entries + Avvia-FrameForge.bat with token present. _ssl.pyd (previously reported corrupt by 7-Zip) reads cleanly (179432 bytes). Cache regression OK (2nd request same Content-Length). launcher-bat endpoint OK. Auth /login + /me regression OK.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correct: using fastapi.responses.Response with explicit Content-Length prevents ingress truncation that occurred with StreamingResponse+BytesIO. Verified header value (9128837) equals bytes received.
context_for_next_testing_agent: ZIP download fix confirmed working. Cache lives in /tmp/forgefps-agent-cache-*.zip - do not delete. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_agent_zip_download.py can be reused as regression.
rca of the issue: Previously /api/agent/download-zip used StreamingResponse(BytesIO) with no Content-Length; ingress/Cloudflare truncated body at ~30% (2794/9128 KB). Fix: return Response(content=payload, headers={'Content-Length': str(len(payload))}). Verified: Content-Length: 9128837 == len(body) == 9128837, testzip()=None.
